title: Shiva IV

{{Quote|This is almost the edge of known space, about as far from the Empire as a body can get. Why'd we come all this way, Princess?|[[Chedaki]], to Leia Organa|<ref name="marvel53" />}}
'''Shiva IV''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] that was noted for its extremely remote location, far from the auspices of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. A world with a history soaked in blood, Shiva IV was marked by generations of warfare between the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Calian Confederacy]] and the [[Twelve Tribes of T'Syriél]], which forged both peoples into extremely formidable fighters. In [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], that conflict was finally brought to an end when [[Calian]] [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Aron Peacebringer]] signed the [[Concordat of Peace]] with [[T'Syriél]] [[Demarch]] [[Kéral Longknife]]. Although the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] attempted to conquer Shiva IV [[3 ABY/Legends|one year later]], Peacebringer and elements of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] repelled the invasion.

==Description==
[[File:ShivaChase.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Calians chase T'Syriél through the Shiva IV "Drylands."]]
Located in the [[Shiva system/Legends|Shiva system]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]],<ref name="atlas" /> Shiva IV was a small [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="marvel54">[[Star Wars (1977) 54|''Star Wars'' (1977) 54]]</ref> that was noted for its extremely remote location, far from the powers that ruled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Shiva IV was covered in vast [[Desert/Legends|deserts]]—which natives called the "Drylands"—and skyscraping [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]], and had conditions suitable to support large civilizations of [[Human]]s and other [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]]. The planet had multiple [[Moon/Legends|moons]], and the moons being down in the sky often made visibility difficult.<ref name="marvel53" /> Shiva IV was located just a short distance from a [[Black hole/Legends|black hole]], formed out of a collapsed [[Star/Legends|star]] just a few [[Meter/Legends|kilometers]] in diameter.<ref name="marvel54" />

==History==
{{Quote|When a starship, or a city, or a planet is destroyed, so many are killed—the numbers are so huge, so incomprehensible—that the Human mind cannot relate to them. But when a ''single'' person dies—at one's hands, before one's eyes—it is like looking into a mirror. A bond has been forged between living and dead. A price is demanded of the victor—the terrible awareness that he or she or it has taken the rarest and most precious thing in all creation: a ''sentient'' life. I was twelve years old when I made my first such kill, and on that day I swore I would bring the killing on Shiva to an end!|Aron Peacebringer|<ref name="marvel54" />}}
[[File:TotallyMetalUmlautCity-MSW54.png|thumb|right|250px|Illyriaqüm, the throne city of the Calian Confederacy, in 3 ABY]]
Shiva IV had a long, bloody history marked by generations of almost constant warfare between two nation-states: the [[Calian Confederacy]] of the planet's Human population, and the [[Twelve Tribes of T'Syriél]], a large and powerful warrior band of the world's native [[T'Syriél]]. Centuries of battle turned both the [[Calian]]s and the T'Syriél into strong martial cultures, and ancient ruins from wars long past dotted Shiva IV's landscape, including [[City/Legends|cities]] that had been annihilated by [[Nuclear weapon/Legends|nuclear bombs]] at some point during the planet's history. By the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], natives of Shiva IV had access to advanced [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|repulsorcraft]], yet still fought using simple, traditional melee weapons in addition to [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]-like weapons.<ref name="marvel53" />

The long-running enmity between the Calians and T'Syriél came to an end in [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], when a warrior known as [[Aron Peacebringer|Aron]]—later called "Peacebringer"—ascended to the title of [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] of the Calian Confederacy, and signed the [[Concordat of Peace]] with [[Kéral Longknife]], [[Demarch]] of the Twelve Tribes. However, it wasn't long until conflict again touched Shiva IV. By [[3 ABY/Legends|the next year]], Shiva IV had fallen into the sights of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which assigned [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Bentilais san Sk'ar]] to conquer the world. Sk'ar planned to do so through subterfuge, hiring both disaffected Calians—including [[Ygal Delois]], the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of Peacebringer's personal guard—and T'Syriél into his army to give the impression to any onlookers that the truce had been broken. In addition, Sk'ar used an [[anti-matter bomb]] to destroy the outlying [[City/Legends|city]] [[K'avor]], hoping to destabilize the planet's governing powers.<ref name="aliens">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/97/indexp4.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 2: To a Traitor Go the Spoils}}</ref>

Not long after, a small [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] task force arrived to investigate what it saw as an unusual amount of Imperial interest in such an outlying system—after their [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was damaged by anti-matter [[micromine]]s left in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the group's leader, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], crashed on Shiva IV alone. Peacebringer and Longknife happened upon Organa in the wastes and rescued her, bringing her back to the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Illyriaqüm]].<ref name="marvel53" /> Sk'ar revealed himself and attempted to attack Illyriaqüm soon afterward, but due to timely assistance from Rebel agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], Sk'ar's plot to destroy the throne city with an anti-matter bomb was foiled and the Imperials chased off the planet.<ref name="marvel54" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|The battle madness of my people is a fearsome, fearful thing—a berserker rage that transforms one into a super-human, nigh-irresistible killing machine—as the Imperials I faced soon learned.|Aron Peacebringer|<ref name="marvel54" />}}
[[File:Calian.png|thumb|left|200px|Members of the Calian Human culture that developed on Shiva IV]]
Shiva IV was inhabited by significant populations of Humans and the planet's native T'Syriél, both of whom spent many years at war with one another for supremacy. The centuries of conflict forged both peoples into formidable warriors—Shiva IV's Human population, called the Calians, were tall, strong and muscular,<ref name="marvel53" /> and were known for entering a "[[Ryastraad|battle madness]]" that turned them into almost unstoppable fighters.<ref name="marvel54" /> For their part the T'Syriél also had a proud warrior heritage, though in the aftermath of the Concordat of Peace, many T'Syriél fell away from the ways of their ancestors and resorted to lives of banditry in the outlands. Both peoples' culture developed with almost no influence or contact with the wider galaxy, and they spoke a [[Calian language]] that was incomprehensible to outsiders and not registered in any [[Translatacomp/Legends|translatacomp]].<ref name="marvel53" />

==Locations==
Calian society on Shiva IV surrounded the great throne city of Illyriaqüm, a large settlement of gleaming buildings and towers highlighted by the Warlord's palace and its [[Great Hall (Shiva IV)|Great Hall]]. However, Calian territory stretched as far as the outlands, with cities like K'avor located upwards as halfway around the world from Illyriaqüm. K'avor had a population of 500,000 as of 3 ABY, although all of its population was killed<ref name="marvel53" /> or captured after the Imperial attack.<ref name="ion">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20081031.html|text="Fists of Ion"|int=Fists of Ion|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Shiva IV first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 53|''Star Wars'' (1977) 53]], written by [[Chris Claremont]] and released in [[1981]]. Most of the art from ''Star Wars'' 53 and [[Star Wars (1977) 54|54]] was not originally intended for a ''[[Star Wars]]'' story—it was originally intended for [[Marvel Comics]]' ''[[Wikipedia:John Carter, Warlord of Mars|John Carter, Warlord of Mars]]'' series, which had been canceled in [[1979]]. Wanting to use 30 pages or so of fully penciled artwork, Marvel had artist [[Carmine Infantino]] adapt the pages for ''Star Wars'', transforming the planet Mars into Shiva IV.<ref name="insider">{{InsiderCite|146|Authors of the Expanded Universe|Authors of the Expanded Universe: Chris Claremont}}</ref>

The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the planet in grid square L-21.<ref name="atlas" />
